revision.comment: Auto-generated page using script written by Immanuelle, either as a placeholder redirect or to make bots recognize the page as made by Immanuelle
revision.text: #redirect[[en:己]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''己''' #（おのれ、おの、おら、な）古語としては一人称（自分を卑下する表現）。現代語では自分自身のこと。 #（おのれ、うぬ、おどれ、な）二人称（相手を低く見る、または罵る表現）。 #（おのれ）悔しい、または怒りの気持ちを表現する[[感動詞]]。通常はかなで書く。 #（き、つちのと）十干の一つ。本項で詳述する。 ---- {{十干}} '''己'''（き、つちのと）は、[[十干]]の6番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[土]]性の陰に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「つちのと」（土の弟）ともいう。
己の字は三本の平行線を形取ったもので、そこから、条理が整然としている状態という意味となる。十干では、植物が充分生長し形が整然としている状態として、6番目に宛てられた。
十干を順位づけに使った場合には、己は6番目の意味となるが、己まで使われることはほとんどない。
なお、字形の似ている已（い、すで）や[[巳]]（し、み）とは、意味も字源も異なる別の字である。これを、「こきの声、おのれつちのと下につき、いすでは半ば、しみは皆つく」と覚えた。
==己を含む干支== *[[己巳]] *[[己卯]] *[[己丑]] *[[己亥]] *[[己酉]] *[[己未]]
{{stub}} [[Category:干支|つちのと]]

己の字は三本の平行線を形取ったもので、そこから、条理が整然としている状態という意味となる。十干では、植物が充分生長し形が整然としている状態として、6番目に宛てられた。

なお、字形の似ている已（い、すで）や[[巳]]（し、み）とは、意味も字源も異なる別の字である。これを、「こきの声、おのれつちのと下につき、いすでは半ば、しみは皆つく」と覚えた。

'''己''' #（おのれ、おの、おら、な）古語としては一人称（自分を卑下する表現）。現代語では自分自身のこと。 #（おのれ、うぬ、おどれ、な）二人称（相手を低く見る、または罵る表現）。 #（おのれ）悔しい、または怒りの気持ちを表現する[[感動詞]]。通常はかなで書く。 #（き、つちのと）十干の一つ。本項で詳述する。 ---- {{十干}} '''己'''（き、つちのと）は、[[十干]]の6番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[土]]性の陰に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「つちのと」（土の弟）ともいう。
己の字は三本の平行線を形取ったもので、そこから、条理が整然としている状態という意味となる。十干では、植物が充分生長し形が整然としている状態として、6番目に宛てられた。
十干を順位づけに使った場合には、己は6番目の意味となるが、己まで使われることはほとんどない。
[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[ヘキサン]]、[[ヘキシル基]]など、[[炭素]]を6つ含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]に付けられる。ただし、[[ビタミン]]などの名称ではFの代わりに使われている。
なお、字形の似ている已（い、すで）や[[巳]]（し、み）とは、意味も字源も異なる別の字である。これを、「こきの声、おのれつちのと下につき、いすでは半ば、しみは皆つく」と覚えた。

{{stub}} [[Category:干支|つちのと]]
[[zh:己]]

{{otheruses|己}} {{十干}} '''己'''（き、つちのと）は、[[十干]]の6番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[土]]性の陰に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「つちのと」（土の弟）ともいう。

十干を順位づけに使った場合には、己は6番目の意味となるが、己まで使われることはほとんどない。
己の年は、[[西暦]]年の下一桁が9の年が己の[[年]]となる。

[[中国語]]の[[化合物]]命名法では、[[ヘキサン]]、[[ヘキシル基]]など、[[炭素]]を6つ含む化合物や[[基|官能基]]に付けられる。
なお、字形の似ている已（い、すで）や[[巳]]（し、み）とは、意味も字源も異なる別の字である。これを、「こきの声、おのれつちのと下につき、いすでは半ば、しみは皆つく」と覚えた。

{{otheruses|己}} {{十干十二支}} '''己'''（き、つちのと）は、[[十干]]の6番目である。[[陰陽五行説]]では[[土]]性の陰に割り当てられており、ここから日本では「つちのと」（土の弟）ともいう。
己の字は三本の平行線を形取ったもので、そこから、条理が整然としている状態という意味となる。十干では、植物が充分生長し形が整然としている状態として、6番目に宛てられた。
十干を順位づけに使った場合には、己は6番目の意味となるが、己まで使われることはほとんどない。
己の年は、[[西暦]]年の下一桁が9の年が己の[[年]]となる。

なお、字形の似ている已（い、すで）や[[巳]]（し、み）とは、意味も字源も異なる別の字である。これを、「こきの声、おのれつちのと下につき、いすでは半ば、しみは皆つく」と覚えた。

revision.text:
{{混同|已|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)}} {{citation needed|'''十干の一つ'''}} {{十干十二支}} '''Ki''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=draft|12=draft}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|hexane|ja|ヘキサン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees).

== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=draft|12=draft}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|hexane|ja|ヘキサン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)==
{| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|己巳|zh|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|己卯|zh|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|己丑|zh|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|己亥|zh|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|己酉|zh|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=draft|12=draft}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|己未|zh|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=draft|12=draft}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix}} * {{intitle}}

{{混同|已|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)}} {{citation needed|'''十干の一つ'''}} {{十干十二支}} '''Ki''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|8=en|ja|十干|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=draft|12=draft}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|hexane|ja|ヘキサン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)==

== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=draft|12=draft}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|hexane|ja|ヘキサン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)==

A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. == Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=draft|12=draft}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|hexane|ja|ヘキサン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)==

== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=draft|12=draft}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|西暦|12=draft|12=draft}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chinese chemical nomenclature|ja|化合物|12=draft|12=draft}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|hexane|ja|ヘキサン|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=draft|12=draft}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)==

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|qq=|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |-
