Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the [[:en:Heavenly Stems|Ten Heavenly Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In [[:en:Chemical compound|Chinese chemical nomenclature]], "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as [[:en:Hexane|hexane]] and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Goat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |} == See Also == * {{prefix|qq=}} * {{intitle|qq=}}

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=rebus character|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature|1=Chemical compound}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane|1=Hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature|1=Chemical compound}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane|1=Hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de terre|de|Erde-Büffel|en|Earth Ox|lt=己丑 (Yin Earth Ox)|lt_ko=기축|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Ox|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Pig|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rooster|ja|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|zh|1=Yin Earth Rooster|2=ja|己酉|ko|기유|fr|Coq de terre|de|Erde-Hahn|en|Earth Rooster|lt=己酉 (Yin Earth Rooster)|lt_ko=기유|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rooster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Goat|ja|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|zh|1=Yin Earth Goat|2=ja|己未|ko|기미|fr|Chèvre de terre|de|Erde-Schaf|en|Earth Goat|lt=己未 (Yin Earth Goat)|lt_ko=기미|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature|1=Chemical compound}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane|1=Hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de terre|de|Erde-Schlange|en|Earth Snake|lt=己巳 (Yin Earth Snake)|lt_ko=기사|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Snake|qq=}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Rabbit|ja|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|zh|1=Yin Earth Rabbit|2=ja|己卯|ko|기묘|fr|Lièvre de terre|de|Erde-Hase|en|Earth Rabbit|lt=己卯 (Yin Earth Rabbit)|lt_ko=기묘|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth Rabbit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- style="text-align:left;font-size:100%" |{{ill|Yin Earth Ox|ja|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|zh|1=Yin Earth Ox|2=ja|己丑|ko|기축|fr|Buffle de Pig|ja|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|zh|1=Yin Earth Pig|2=ja|己亥|ko|기해|fr|Cochon de terre|de|Erde-Schwein|en|Earth Pig|lt=己亥 (Yin Earth Pig)|lt_ko=기해|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Earth {{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Tsuchinoto''' (己) is the 6th of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems|1=Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=746338a778c1326c28e064dfc238d5f40d5be0b4b34cbd5a628d4c817af3433cJmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD01NTc5MiZlbGVtZW50X2lkPTcxNDQx&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=39bc19b09043852cb3f39659fd413e4d1ce12d6b5eb2c08296574682c71f8352JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qYXBhbmVzZS13b3Jkcy5vcmcvZW4va2Fuamkvdmlldy83OTQ&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=b8a08bad34a4a3ecb849614d125872ad9b7a09f4bddf3e4eb32624f3436bced5JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Tsuchinoto+(%e5%b7%b1)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubmlob25nb21hc3Rlci5jb20vamFwYW5lc2UvZGljdGlvbmFyeS93b3JkLzU1NjM4L2tpLXRzdWNoaW5vdG8tJUU1JUI3JUIxLSVFMyU4MSU4RC0lRTMlODElQTQlRTMlODElQTElRTMlODElQUUlRTMlODElQTg&ntb=1</ref>
== Pronunciations: 己 (Stem 6) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Jǐ * Korean: Gi (기) * Vietnamese: Kỷ * Japanese: ** On’yomi: Ki (き) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Tsuchinoto (つちのと, 土の弟 – “Younger Earth”)
== Overview == * The origin of the character "己" has been the subject of various hypotheses, but no definitive theory exists.<ref>{{Citation| author=Ji Xusheng| year=2014| title=Shuowen New Evidence| location=Taipei| publisher=Yiwen Publishing House| isbn=978-957-520-168-5| page=964|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Lin Zhiqiang et al.| year=2017| title=Annotations on "Wenyuan"| location=Beijing| publisher=Chinese Academy of Social Sciences Press| isbn=978-7-5203-0419-1| page=116|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Citation| author=Ge Liang| year=2022| title=Re-discovering Chinese Characters: From Old Knowledge to New Insights| location=Shanghai| publisher=Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Press| isbn=978-7-5479-2884-4| page=106|qq=}}</ref> The use of "己" to denote the sixth position in the Ten Stems is a case of {{ill|phonetic loan|ja|仮借|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/phonetic loan|1=Loangraph|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10888485}}. * Although visually similar to 已 (i, "already") and {{ill|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|ja|巳|1=Snake (Zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|2=ja|3=巳|1=Snake (zodiac)|lt=Snake (巳)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/巳|13=User:Immanuelle/Snake (zodiac)|qq=}} (shi, "serpent"), these are entirely different characters with distinct meanings and origins. A mnemonic for this is "Ko-ki sounds for 'tsuchinoto', different from 'isude' and 'shimi'." * When used for Ranking, "己" signifies the sixth position, though it is rarely used beyond this context. * Years ending in the digit 9 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era|1=Common Era}} are Ki years. The alternate name for Ki years is '''Tuwei''' (or '''Tu Wei''').<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Names and Explanations of the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Chemical compound|en|Chemical compound|WD=Q11173|lt=Chinese chemical nomenclature|1=Chemical compound}}, "Ki" is used in names for compounds containing six carbon atoms, such as {{ill|Hexane|en|Hexane|WD=Q150440|lt=hexane|1=Hexane}} and {{ill|hexyl group|ja|ヘキシル基|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hexyl group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The opposite counterpart in the Ten Stems cycle is {{ill|Kō|ja|甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|lt=Kinoe (甲)|2=ja|3=甲|4=zh|5=甲|1=Kinoe (甲)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinoe (甲)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|恵方|3=歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 歳徳神}} (Eho) for Ki is slightly east of northeast (75 degrees). ==Sexagenary cycles containing Tsuchinoto (己)== {{wikt|己}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Earth Snake|ja|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|zh|1=Yin Earth Snake|己巳|ko|기사|fr|Serpent de
